flashcards.front: What is a nested class in Java?
flashcards.back: A nested class is a class defined within another class. It provides logical grouping of related classes and enhances encapsulation by allowing a class to be a member of another class.

flashcards.front: How many types of nested classes are there in Java?
flashcards.back: There are four types: static nested classes, non-static inner classes, local inner classes, and anonymous inner classes.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between a static nested class and an inner class?
flashcards.back: A static nested class cannot access non-static members of the outer class directly, while a non-static inner class can access all members (including private) of the outer class.

flashcards.front: How do you instantiate a static nested class?
flashcards.back: Use outerClassName.staticNestedClassName object = new outerClassName.staticNestedClassName(); No outer class instance is required.

flashcards.front: How do you instantiate a non-static inner class?
flashcards.back: Create an instance of outer class first, then: outerClassName outer = new outerClassName(); outerClassName.innerClassName inner = outer.new innerClassName();

flashcards.front: What is a local inner class in Java?
flashcards.back: A local inner class is defined within a method, constructor, or initializer block. It can only be accessed within that scope and can access final or effectively final variables.

flashcards.front: What is an anonymous inner class?
flashcards.back: An anonymous inner class is a class without a name, defined and instantiated in a single expression. It is typically used to override methods of a class or implement an interface on the fly.

flashcards.front: Can nested classes have access modifiers?
flashcards.back: Yes, nested classes can have public, private, protected, or default (package-private) access modifiers to control their visibility.

flashcards.front: What is shadowing in nested classes?
flashcards.back: Shadowing occurs when a nested class member has the same name as an outer class member. The inner class member shadows the outer class member within the nested class scope.

flashcards.front: How can an inner class reference its outer class instance?
flashcards.back: Use outerClassName.this to reference the outer class instance. For example, OuterClass.this to access outer class members.

flashcards.front: What is the syntax to access static members of outer class in a static nested class?
flashcards.back: Simply use the static member name directly or reference it through the outer class name (OuterClass.staticMember).

flashcards.front: Why use nested classes in Java?
flashcards.back: Benefits include: logical grouping of classes, increased encapsulation, enhanced code readability and maintainability, and the ability to access private members of the outer class.

flashcards.front: Can an interface be nested inside a class?
flashcards.back: Yes, interfaces can be nested within classes. Such interfaces are implicitly static. Similarly, classes can be nested inside interfaces.

flashcards.front: What is the default modifier for a nested class?
flashcards.back: If no access modifier is specified, the nested class has default (package-private) access, meaning it is accessible within the same package.

flashcards.front: Can a static nested class be instantiated from outside the outer class?
flashcards.back: Yes, a static nested class can be instantiated from anywhere with proper access, using: new OuterClass.StaticNestedClass();
